Phase 3: Model Selection and Evaluation¶

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
In [2]:
from google.colab import files
uploaded = files.upload()
Upload widget is only available when the cell has been executed in the current browser session. Please rerun this cell to enable. 
Saving sec_data_clean.csv to sec_data_clean.csv
In [3]:
dataset = pd.read_csv('sec_data_clean.csv', delimiter=',')
dataset.head()

5 rows × 77 columns

Step 2: Shuffle the rows¶

In [4]:
dataset['class'].unique()
Out[4]:
array([1, 0])
In [5]:
dataset = np.genfromtxt('sec_data_clean.csv', delimiter=',', skip_header=1)
np.random.shuffle(dataset)
In [6]:
print(dataset.shape)

(61069, 77)

Step 3: Split into Training and Validation Set¶

I'll do an 80-20 split

In [7]:
index_20_percent = int(0.2 * len(dataset[:, 0]))
print(index_20_percent)

12213
In [8]:
XVALIDATION = dataset[:index_20_percent, 1:]  # All but the first column
YVALIDATION = dataset[:index_20_percent, 0]  # Only the first column

XTRAIN = dataset[index_20_percent:, 1:]      # All but the first column
YTRAIN = dataset[index_20_percent:, 0]      # Only the first column
In [9]:
print(XVALIDATION.shape)
print(YVALIDATION.shape)
print(XTRAIN.shape)
print(YTRAIN.shape)

(12213, 76)
(12213,)
(48856, 76)
(48856,)

print(YVALIDATION[0])
print(XTRAIN[0])
print(YTRAIN[0])

  • Check the distribution of the output class to make sure it is similar between the two cases
In [11]:
plt.hist(YTRAIN)
plt.ylabel('Ouput Class')
plt.show()

plt.hist(YVALIDATION)
plt.ylabel('Ouput Class')
plt.show()
In [12]:
plt.hist(XTRAIN[:, 0])
plt.ylabel('Input Features')
plt.show()

First, we do a random baseline classifier for the training and validation sets¶

In [13]:
# Use a majority class classifier for the baseline accuracy
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score
from sklearn.dummy import DummyClassifier
dummy_clf = DummyClassifier(strategy="most_frequent")
dummy_clf.fit(XTRAIN, YTRAIN)
pred_train = dummy_clf.predict(XTRAIN)
accuracy_train = accuracy_score(YTRAIN, pred_train)
print('Accuracy (Training): %.2f%%' % (accuracy_train * 100.0))
predic_val = dummy_clf.predict(XVALIDATION)
accuracy_val = accuracy_score(YVALIDATION, predic_val)
print('Accuracy (Validation): %.2f%%' % (accuracy_val * 100.0))

Accuracy (Training): 55.38%
Accuracy (Validation): 55.95%
In [44]:
# Output the scores - f1, precision, recall, accuracy
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, precision_score, recall_score
print(f'F1_score: {f1_score(YVALIDATION, predic_val):.4f}')
print(f'Precision: {precision_score(YVALIDATION, predic_val) * 100: .4f}%')
print(f'Recall: {recall_score(YVALIDATION, predic_val) * 100: .4f}%')
print(f'Accuracy: {accuracy_score(YVALIDATION, predic_val) * 100: .4f}%')

F1_score: 0.7175
Precision:  55.9486%
Recall:  100.0000%
Accuracy:  55.9486%

Start With a logistic regression model¶

In [14]:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense
In [15]:
model = Sequential()
model.add(Dense(1, input_shape=(XTRAIN.shape[1],), activation='sigmoid'))
model.compile(optimizer='rmsprop', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
In [18]:
from keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping, ModelCheckpoint
callbackA = ModelCheckpoint(filepath='best_model.keras', monitor='val_loss', save_best_only=True, verbose=1)
callbackB = EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', mode = 'min', patience=20, verbose=1)

history = model.fit(XTRAIN, YTRAIN, epochs=1000, batch_size=512, validation_data=(XVALIDATION, YVALIDATION), callbacks=[callbackA, callbackB])

model.load_weights('best_model.keras')
scores = model.evaluate(XTRAIN, YTRAIN)
print(scores)
print(f"\n{model.metrics_names[1]} Training: {scores[1] * 100:.2f}%")

In [34]:
# Evaluate using accuracy, precision, recall, and f1
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score
accuracy = accuracy_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
precision = precision_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
recall = recall_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
f1 = f1_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
print('Accuracy: %.2f%%' % (accuracy * 100.0))
print('Precision: %.2f%%' % (precision * 100.0))
print('Recall: %.2f%%' % (recall * 100.0))
print(f'F1 Score: {f1:.2f}')

Accuracy: 83.03%
Precision: 88.95%
Recall: 79.56%
F1 Score: 0.84

Improve the model: Neural Network 2-1¶

In [23]:
model1 = Sequential()
model1.add(Dense(2, input_shape=(XTRAIN.shape[1],), activation='relu'))
model1.add(Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))
model1.compile(optimizer='rmsprop', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
In [24]:

In [25]:
from keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping, ModelCheckpoint
callbackA = ModelCheckpoint(filepath='best_model.keras', monitor='val_loss', save_best_only=True, verbose=1)
callbackB = EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', mode = 'min', patience=20, verbose=1)
history1 = model1.fit(XTRAIN, YTRAIN, epochs=1000, batch_size=512, validation_data=(XVALIDATION, YVALIDATION), callbacks=[callbackA, callbackB])

plt.plot(history1.history['val_accuracy'])

plt.xlabel('Epoch')
plt.legend(['Train', 'Validation'], loc='lower right')
plt.show()

In [35]:
# Get all the metrics
accuracy = accuracy_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
precision = precision_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
recall = recall_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
f1 = f1_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
print('Accuracy: %.2f%%' % (accuracy * 100.0))
print('Precision: %.2f%%' % (precision * 100.0))
print('Recall: %.2f%%' % (recall * 100.0))
print(f'F1_score: {f1: .2f}')

Accuracy: 83.03%
Precision: 88.95%
Recall: 79.56%
F1_score:  0.84

Let's try a 4-2-1 Model¶

In [36]:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense
# Run a new model -> 4-2-1
model3 = Sequential()
model3.add(Dense(4, input_shape=(XTRAIN.shape[1],), activation='relu'))
model3.add(Dense(2, activation='relu'))
model3.add(Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))
model3.compile(optimizer='rmsprop', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
from keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping, ModelCheckpoint

callbackA = ModelCheckpoint(filepath='best_model.keras', monitor='val_loss', save_best_only=True, verbose=1)
callbackB = EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', mode = 'min', patience=20, verbose=1)

history3 = model3.fit(XTRAIN, YTRAIN, epochs=1000, batch_size=512, validation_data=(XVALIDATION, YVALIDATION), callbacks=[callbackA, callbackB])

# Check learning curves
print(history3.params)
plt.plot(history3.history['accuracy'])
plt.plot(history3.history['val_accuracy'])
plt.title('Model Accuracy')
plt.ylabel('Accuracy')
plt.xlabel('Epoch')
plt.legend(['Train', 'Validation'], loc='lower right')
plt.show()

{'verbose': 1, 'epochs': 1000, 'steps': 96}
In [38]:
# Plot RUC and AUC curves for the model
from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve, auc
prediction = model3.predict(XVALIDATION)
fpr, tpr, thresholds = roc_curve(YVALIDATION, prediction)
roc_auc = auc(fpr, tpr)
plt.figure()
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, color='darkorange', lw=2, label='ROC curve (area = %0.2f)' % roc_auc)
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], color='navy', lw=2, linestyle='--')
plt.xlim([0.0, 1.0])
plt.ylim([0.0, 1.05])
plt.fill_between(fpr, tpr, alpha=0.2, color='orange', label='AUC area')
plt.xlabel('False Positive Rate')
plt.ylabel('True Positive Rate')
plt.title('Receiver Operating Characteristic')
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.show()

In [41]:
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score
# get all the metrics
prediction = model3.predict(XVALIDATION)
accuracy = accuracy_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
precision = precision_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
recall = recall_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
f1 = f1_score(YVALIDATION, prediction.round())
print('Accuracy: %.2f%%' % (accuracy * 100.0))
print('Precision: %.2f%%' % (precision * 100.0))
print('Recall: %.2f%%' % (recall * 100.0))
print(f"F1  score: {f1: .2f}")

382/382 [==============================] - 0s 896us/step

Phase 4: Feature Importance¶

In [ ]:
# Load the weights from model4
model3.load_weights('best_model.keras')
In [ ]:
print(model3.layers)

[<keras.src.layers.core.dense.Dense object at 0x7b4871c7e1a0>, <keras.src.layers.core.dense.Dense object at 0x7b4871c7cee0>, <keras.src.layers.core.dense.Dense object at 0x7b4871c7dae0>]
  • Train one model for each column in the Features (XTrain)
  • I don't want to run a new model for each feature -> I want to use model4
In [ ]:
XTRAIN.shape
Out[ ]:
(48856, 76)
In [ ]:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential, Model
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Input, Dense
from tensorflow.keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping, ModelCheckpoint
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score
features = [
    'cap-diameter', 'does-bruise-or-bleed', 'stem-height', 'stem-width', 'has-ring',
    'cap-shape_b', 'cap-shape_c', 'cap-shape_f', 'cap-shape_o', 'cap-shape_p', 'cap-shape_s', 'cap-shape_x',
    'cap-color_b', 'cap-color_e', 'cap-color_g', 'cap-color_k', 'cap-color_l', 'cap-color_n', 'cap-color_o', 'cap-color_p', 'cap-color_r', 'cap-color_u', 'cap-color_w', 'cap-color_y',
    'gill-attachment_a', 'gill-attachment_d', 'gill-attachment_e', 'gill-attachment_f', 'gill-attachment_p', 'gill-attachment_s', 'gill-attachment_x',
    'gill-color_b', 'gill-color_e', 'gill-color_f', 'gill-color_g', 'gill-color_k', 'gill-color_n', 'gill-color_o', 'gill-color_p', 'gill-color_r', 'gill-color_u', 'gill-color_w', 'gill-color_y',
    'stem-color_b', 'stem-color_e', 'stem-color_f', 'stem-color_g', 'stem-color_k', 'stem-color_l', 'stem-color_n', 'stem-color_o', 'stem-color_p', 'stem-color_r', 'stem-color_u', 'stem-color_w', 'stem-color_y',
    'ring-type_e', 'ring-type_f', 'ring-type_g', 'ring-type_l', 'ring-type_m', 'ring-type_p', 'ring-type_r', 'ring-type_z',
    'habitat_d', 'habitat_g', 'habitat_h', 'habitat_l', 'habitat_m', 'habitat_p', 'habitat_u', 'habitat_w',
    'season_a', 'season_s', 'season_u', 'season_w'
]
results = []
for i in range(XTRAIN.shape[1]):
    fet = features[i]
    XTRAIN_single_feature = XTRAIN[:, i].reshape(-1, 1)
    XVALIDATION_single_feature = XVALIDATION[:, i].reshape(-1, 1)

    model = Sequential()
    model.add(Dense(4, input_shape=(1,), activation='relu'))
    model.add(Dense(2, activation='relu'))
    model.add(Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    model.compile(optimizer='rmsprop', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
    # Add callbacks
    callbackA = ModelCheckpoint(filepath='best_model.keras', monitor='val_loss', save_best_only=True, verbose=1)
    callbackB = EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', mode = 'min', patience=20, verbose=1)
    model.fit(XTRAIN_single_feature, YTRAIN, epochs=1000, batch_size=512, validation_data=(XVALIDATION_single_feature, YVALIDATION), callbacks=[callbackA, callbackB])

    train_accuracy = model.evaluate(XTRAIN_single_feature, YTRAIN, verbose=0)[1]
    val_accuracy = model.evaluate(XVALIDATION_single_feature, YVALIDATION, verbose=0)[1]

    results.append((fet, train_accuracy, val_accuracy))

results_df = pd.DataFrame(results, columns=['Feature', 'Train Accuracy', 'Validation Accuracy'])
results_df.sort_values(by='Validation Accuracy', ascending=True, inplace=True)
results_df.reset_index(drop=True, inplace=True)
results_df

In [ ]:
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense

val_accuracies = []

features = results_df['Feature'].to_list()

all_feature_indices = results_df.index.to_list()


# Iterate over rows to remove features one by one
for i, (idx, row) in enumerate(results_df.iterrows()):
    fet = row['Feature']
    print(f'Iteration {i + 1}, Removing Feature {row["Feature"]}')

    # Create remaining features list correctly
    if i < len(all_feature_indices) - 1:
        remaining_features = all_feature_indices[i + 1:]
    else:
        # Skip iteration when no features are left
        print("No more features to remove.")
        break

    # Select only the remaining features from the training and validation data
    XTRAIN_reduced = XTRAIN[:, remaining_features]
    XVALIDATION_reduced = XVALIDATION[:, remaining_features]

    # Build and compile the model
    model_fin = Sequential()
    model_fin.add(Dense(4, input_shape=(XTRAIN_reduced.shape[1],), activation='relu'))  # Adjust input shape
    model_fin.add(Dense(2, activation='relu'))
    model_fin.add(Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))
    model_fin.compile(optimizer='rmsprop', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])

    callbackA = ModelCheckpoint(filepath='best_model.keras', monitor='val_loss', save_best_only=True, verbose=1)
    callbackB = EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', mode = 'min', patience=20, verbose=1)

    # Train the model on the reduced feature set
    model_fin.fit(
        XTRAIN_reduced, YTRAIN,
        epochs=256, batch_size=512,
        validation_data=(XVALIDATION_reduced, YVALIDATION),
        verbose=1,
        callbacks=[callbackA, callbackB]
    )

    # get validation accuracy
    val_accuracy = model_fin.evaluate(XVALIDATION_reduced, YVALIDATION, verbose=0)[1]

    # Append the validation accuracy to the list
    val_accuracies.append((idx+1,val_accuracy))

In [ ]:
# Run stem-width by itself

model = Sequential()
model.add(Dense(4, input_shape=(1,), activation='relu'))
model.add(Dense(2, activation='relu'))
model.add(Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

model.compile(optimizer='rmsprop', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])

model.fit(XTRAIN[:, -1].reshape(-1,1), YTRAIN, epochs=256, batch_size=512, validation_data=(XVALIDATION[:, -1].reshape(-1,1), YVALIDATION))
val_accuracy = model.evaluate(XVALIDATION[:, -1].reshape(-1,1), YVALIDATION, verbose=0)[1]

val_accuracies.append((76,val_accuracy))

sorted_features = results_df['Feature'].tolist()
print(sorted_features)

final_results_df = pd.DataFrame(val_accuracies, columns=['Feature Index', 'Validation Accuracy'])
final_results_df['Feature'] = sorted_features
final_results_df.tail()
Out[ ]:
Feature Index Validation Accuracy Feature
71 72 0.557603 habitat_l
72 73 0.575698 cap-color_b
73 74 0.570949 gill-attachment_e
74 75 0.552854 stem-color_w
75 76 0.552854 stem-width
In [ ]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Extract feature indices and validation accuracies
feature_indices, accuracies = zip(*val_accuracies)

# Create a bar plot (histogram-like representation)
plt.figure(figsize=(14,8))
plt.bar(feature_indices, accuracies, color='skyblue', edgecolor='black', width=0.6)

# Add labels, title, and grid
plt.xlabel('Removed Feature Index', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Validation Accuracy', fontsize=12)
plt.ylim(0.5, 1)
plt.title('Validation Accuracy After Feature Removal', fontsize=14)

plt.xticks(feature_indices, fontsize=10, rotation=45)  # Rotate x-axis labels if needed
plt.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)

# Show the plot
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\n Feature index mapping:")
print(final_results_df[['Feature Index', 'Feature']].to_string(index = False))
